title: Assembly and method for installing a subsea cable
abstract: An assembly (400) for installing a subsea cable (304), the assembly comprising a submersible apparatus (200) and a submersible cable holder (300) comprising the subsea cable, the subsea cable being connectable to the submersible apparatus through a connector (202) for transmission of electric power and/or data to the submersible apparatus; wherein the assembly is configured to install the subsea cable while the submersible apparatus is electrically powered through said subsea cable. And a method for installing a subsea cable (304) with a submersible apparatus (200), the method comprising: supplying electric power and/or transmitting data through the subsea cable to the submersible apparatus; and laying the subsea cable in a sea with the submersible apparatus.
claims: Claims 1. An assembly (400) for installing a subsea cable (304), the assembly (400) comprising: a submersible apparatus (200) comprising a deck (201 ), at least one connector (202), and moving means (208); and a submersible cable holder (300) comprising the subsea cable (304), the subsea cable (304) being connectable to the submersible apparatus (200) through the at least one connector (202) for transmission of electric power and/or data to the submersible apparatus (200), and the submersible cable holder (300) being configured to be loaded on and unloaded from the deck (201 ); wherein the assembly (400) is configured to install the subsea cable (304) while the submersible apparatus (200) is electrically powered through the subsea cable (304). 2. The assembly (400) of claim 1 , wherein a first end of the subsea cable (304) is connected to a power source of a facility (101 , 102) so that electric power and/or data may be delivered to the submersible apparatus (200) upon connection of a second end of the subsea cable (304) to the connector (202) of the submersible apparatus (200). 3. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) is configured to transmit and receive data through the subsea cable (304). 4. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises means (206) for digging a trench. 5. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises at least one battery (207), the submersible apparatus (200) being configured to obtain electric power from the at least one battery (207). 6. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises means for wireless data transmission and reception, the submersible apparatus (200) being configured to transmit and receive data through said means, and/or the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises a connector (215) for connection of a cable for data and power transmission to other devices. 7. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises means (203) for manipulating at least one of: the cable (304), a cable connector, a cable accessory, or an obstacle on a seabed. 8. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein at least one of the submersible apparatus (200) and the submersible cable holder (300) comprises means for changing a buoyancy of the submersible apparatus (200) or the submersible cable holder (300), respectively. 9. The assembly (400) of any of the preceding claims, wherein the submersible apparatus (200) further comprises an acoustic positioning system for sensing subsea acoustic beacons. 10. A method for installing a subsea cable (304) with a submersible apparatus (200), the method comprising: supplying electric power and/or transmitting data through the subsea cable (304) to the submersible apparatus (200); and laying the subsea cable (304) in a sea with the submersible apparatus (200). 1 1 . The method of claim 10, wherein laying the subsea cable (304) in the sea with the submersible apparatus (200) comprises laying the subsea cable (304) on a seabed (600). 12. The method of claim 10, further comprising digging a trench (604) in a seabed (600) with the submersible apparatus (200), and wherein laying the subsea cable (304) in the sea with the submersible apparatus (200) comprises laying the subsea cable (304) in the trench (604). 13. The method of any of claims 10 to 12, wherein a first end of the subsea cable (304) is connected to a power source of a facility (101 , 102) and a second end is connected to the submersible apparatus (200). 14. The method of any of claims 10 to 13, further comprising loading a submersible cable holder (300) on the submersible apparatus (200), the submersible cable holder comprising the subsea cable (304) preferably in a rolled form. 15. The method of any of claims 10 to 14, further comprising transmitting data to and/or receiving data from the submersible apparatus (200) through the subsea cable (304). 16. The method of any of claims 10 to 15, further comprising introducing part of the subsea cable (304) in a carrying means (615,616,620), the carrying means (615,616,620) comprising two unassembled tubes (615,616) being configured to be hoisted from a facility (601 ,61 1 ). 17. A system for installing a subsea cable (304), the system comprising: a carrying means (615,616,620) configured to receive the subsea cable (304) while the carrying means (615,616,620) is contacting a seafloor (600); and a facility (601 ,61 1 ) configured to hoist the carrying means (615,616,620). 18. The system of claim 17, wherein: the carrying means (615,616,620) comprises at least one unassembled tube (615,616) configured to be open in a first part (616) and a second part (615), wherein the first part (616) is configured to receive the subsea cable (304) while the first part (616) is contacting the seafloor (600); and the facility (601 , 61 1 ) is configured to hoist the first part (616). 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the second part (615) is configured to attach to the facility (601 , 61 1 ), and to receive the first part (616) upon hoisting the first part (616) thereby joining together the first part (616) and the second part (615). 20. The system of any of claims 18-19, wherein the carrying means (615,616) comprises two unassembled tubes (615,616) configured to receive the subsea cable (304) while the first part (616) is contacting the seafloor (600) such that the subsea cable (304) forms a U shape between the two unassembled tubes (615,616). 21 . The system of any of claims 18-20, wherein each unassembled tube (615,616) of the carrying means (615,616) comprises an open cross-section. 22. The system of any of claims 18-21 , wherein each unassembled tube (615,616) of the carrying means (615,616) comprises a side open lengthwise. 23. The system of claim 17, wherein the carrying means (615,616,620) comprises a hollow device (620) with a semi-circular shape and configured to receive the subsea cable (304) while the first part (616) is contacting the seafloor (600). 24. The system of any of claims 17-23, wherein the facility (601 ,61 1 ) comprises a lifting mechanism (617) and an elongated flexible element (630) connected to the lifting mechanism (617), and wherein the carrying means (615,616,620) is configured to be secured to the elongated flexible element (630).
